asymmetry: NHS staff are chemically sedating dementia patients and physically preventing them from leaving their beds — not as a last resort, but routinely.
hook: The NHS is sedating dementia patients to keep them quiet.
core_fact: The first-ever national review of NHS dementia care, spanning 18 months of direct observation, found patients are routinely sedated and physically restrained in bed — a practice investigators described as being 'held captive'.
villain: NHS hospital trusts and the care system that has allowed this to become standard, undisclosed practice across the country.
share_trigger: Did you see this? They found NHS staff are basically drugging dementia patients to stop them being a nuisance — and it's been going on everywhere.
search_query: NHS dementia patients sedated restrained care homes UK investigation 2024 2025
